author: Besting
text: REVISED 2026-06-06: a fresh landing's substrate is SEEDED by copying the app-level default-config/ folder into balls/config (not hand-built defaults). The seed carries tracker/delivery/builtin plugin wiring, so they are ordinary landing entries — no runtime plugin defaults. (Seed mechanism itself = new sibling task; this task just creates the landing/store branches + binding and invokes the seed.) spec bl-2e26 §1/§12.

author: Aurally
text: Coordination (2026-06-06, after bl-35f7 landed c6b9569): the task-op DISPATCH that resolves the terminus + builds the §7 binding now lives in src/mutate.rs (bl-35f7, the §9 mutating-verb wiring) — it post-dates this ball's 'WHAT amends' list (bl-e755/bl-5c65/bl-be5f/bl-4450), so don't miss it. mutate::run currently does ALL of the following against the single clone.operating()/STATE_BRANCH and must be split to the two-branch model: (1) Registry::at(&operating).resolve(op,phase) — the plugin chain lives on the LANDING (config/plugins, §2), so resolve from the landing checkout; (2) the CHANGE worktree + Git::at(&operating).seal — task ops seal onto the STORE (tasks_branch), so the terminus is the store checkout; (3) it builds the binding via checkout::binding(operating, invocation, origin_of(operating), STATE_BRANCH). NOTE: bl-35f7 made checkout::binding + checkout::origin_of pub(crate) and mutate REUSES them — so the new {remote,tasks_branch,store,landing,invocation_path} binding has ONE construction point (checkout::binding); update its signature there and fix the two call sites (checkout prime/sync + mutate). So 'task ops seal onto the store' = editing mutate.rs, not just substrate/engine.

author: Kenning
text: Coordination (2026-06-06, after bl-154f landed 9e65ab8): the §13 diffless post facts couple to this ball's 'retire operating/ + lifecycle seal targets' work. Engine::diffless_inner (src/lifecycle.rs:233-236) reads self.terminus.head() BEFORE and AFTER pre to bracket previous_commit/commit for the sync/prime post wire. Two corrections fall out of this ball: (1) TERMINUS TARGET — those head reads must resolve the STORE branch (tasks_branch) HEAD, since §13 specs the facts as 'the store branch HEAD before and after the op'. run_chain currently builds terminus = Git::at(clone.operating()); when operating/ is retired, point the diffless terminus at the store checkout so the facts bracket the store, not the landing/config branch. (2) FRESH-CLONE NULL — §13: 'previous_commit ... null on a fresh-clone prime where nothing pre-existed.' Once the store branch can be unborn independently of the founded landing, head()-before-pre will FAIL on an unborn ref and abort the op (OpError::Terminus). It must degrade to None, and previous_commit must be representable as null: Sealed.previous_commit (lifecycle.rs:52) and SealFacts.previous_commit (wire.rs:84) are non-optional &str today; make them Option<&str> — the wire Payload.previous_commit (wire.rs:110) is ALREADY Option, so only the Sealed→SealFacts→wire path forcing Some(...) (wire.rs:163) needs to thread the None through. Today this is unreachable (single 'balls' branch; substrate::found commits once before the engine runs, so HEAD always exists), so bl-154f is correct as landed — these are the deltas that go live when the store ref can be unborn.

author: Xxxii
text:
DELIVERY DOCTOR COUPLING (bl-0ec3, landed b65d3ef) — include in the '§16 doctor corrections' this ball blocks. The delivery §16 hook (src/delivery_doctor.rs + bl-delivery doctor()) was built against the OLD model. Two things to fix when this lands:
1. cwd: doctor's diffless read does claimed_ids(env::current_dir(), actor) → reads cwd/tasks/. After operating/ retires, the read-op cwd must be the STORE checkout root (whose top-level tasks/ folder then resolves). Verify the diffless cwd is set to the store checkout, not the tasks/ dir itself.
2. binding: delivery doctor uses only wire.binding.invocation_path (survives the new binding) — UNLIKE bl-3d75's prime.post hook, which reads wire.binding.operating (REMOVED by this ball). bl-3d75's hook needs the rename to store/landing; my doctor hook does not.

SEPARATE coupling (NOT this ball — §11 <id>-<claimant> keying, if/when implemented): worktree_path gains a claimant component → delivery_doctor's partition (bare-id claimed set vs materialized dir-names) breaks (every worktree reads as orphan, every claim as missing). delivery_doctor must adopt the composite key in lockstep with worktree_path. This is also the spec's fix for the multi-actor orphan false-positive noted on bl-0ec3.

author: Rescued
text: Implementation plan (Rescued): physical realization = ONE git repo per clone, two branches in it — landing 'balls/config' (main worktree at clones/<enc>/config/, holds config/ folder) + store tasks_branch default 'balls/tasks' (linked worktree at clones/<enc>/tasks/, holds tasks/ folder). 'refs coincide' (tasks_branch==balls/config) ⇒ store path = landing path, one worktree, both folders. Edits: layout(operating→landing/store) · wire Binding{remote,tasks_branch,store,landing,invocation_path}+previous_commit:Option · substrate found two branches/worktrees · mutate seal store/resolve chain from landing · lifecycle diffless terminus=store head+unborn→None · checkout prime founds both/sync targets store · config read from landing(no trail walk)/branch→tasks_branch · install seal landing · tracker+delivery+doctor binding/cwd carry. Trail-layering correctness + sync store semantics + tracker remote resolution + doctor remain to siblings bl-30e2/bl-9329/bl-cd21/bl-a38e; this ball carries them only to green.

author: Rescued
text: IMPLEMENTED (Rescued). Two-branch substrate live: substrate::found makes balls/config (landing, main worktree) + balls/tasks (store, linked orphan worktree via mktree/commit-tree plumbing, tracked tasks/.gitkeep); layout operating()→landing()/store(); wire Binding{remote,tasks_branch,store,landing,invocation_path}; mutate resolves chain from LANDING + seals onto STORE (Git::at(store)) via the single checkout::binding ctor; checkout prime founds both/sync targets store; lifecycle diffless terminus=store (Kenning corr#1); config reads landing (no trail), branch→tasks_branch (default balls/tasks); doctor audits landing(registry/config)+store(blockers). RETIRED: trail.rs+trail_tests.rs deleted, pointer write/clear cut, --center/--stealth removed. Tracker payload mechanically renamed operating→store/branch→tasks_branch to keep the wire⟷tracker contract (e2e dispatch/tracker tests exercise the real subprocess). Gates: 282 tests pass, clippy 0, all files <300 lines, 100% coverage (1625/1625). DEFERRED to named siblings: Kenning corr#2 (Option previous_commit) unreachable in f970 (found commits store before engine, HEAD always exists) → bl-9329/cd21 when store ref can be unborn; delivery prime.post still reads wire.binding.operating (now absent) → bl-9773 (its named owner; delivery not in default wiring so pipeline unaffected); config-layering rules→bl-30e2; sync remote semantics→bl-9329; tracker remote resolution/pointer removal→bl-cd21; install seal-onto-landing wiring→bl-3dd9; seed default-config/ folder→bl-4e14.
